checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is nonce incremented before message execution?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Trace process_transaction to verify increment_nonce(state, sender) is called before process_message_call. 2. Test for reentrancy scenarios where same nonce could be reused. 3. Verify ordering is correct for cross-transaction protection within same block.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-30, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-006. Late nonce increment enables reentrancy attacks.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are gas fees deducted from sender balance before message execution?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Trace process_transaction to verify max gas fees are deducted before process_message_call. 2. Test if sender can spend the balance during execution to avoid paying gas. 3. Verify both effective_gas_fee and blob_gas_fee are deducted upfront.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-31, Trust Boundary: tb-006. Late fee deduction enables fee-evasion exploit.

checklist_item.title: Is unused gas refunded at the correct effective_gas_price?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify refund calculation: gas_left * effective_gas_price. 2. Test for incorrect price usage (base fee vs effective price). 3. Calculate expected refund and compare to actual refund. 4. Test with various gas price configurations.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-32, Trust Boundary: tb-006. Incorrect refund affects ETH supply accuracy.

checklist_item.title: Does coinbase receive only the priority fee portion (not base fee)?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify coinbase payment: tx_gas_used * (effective_gas_price - base_fee_per_gas). 2. Confirm base fee is burned, not paid to coinbase. 3. Test for incorrect full payment of effective gas price. 4. Verify EIP-1559 deflationary mechanism is correctly implemented.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-33, Trust Boundary: tb-006. Base fee to coinbase breaks EIP-1559 economics.

checklist_item.title: Are accounts marked for deletion destroyed after transaction execution?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Trigger SELFDESTRUCT to mark account for deletion. 2. Verify account is destroyed after transaction execution. 3. Test if accounts_to_delete set is properly processed. 4. Verify state root reflects account deletion.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-34, Trust Boundary: tb-006. Failure to delete causes state trie divergence.

checklist_item.title: Does beacon roots system transaction execute before user transactions?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify apply_body calls process_unchecked_system_transaction(BEACON_ROOTS_ADDRESS) first. 2. Test if user transactions can access expected beacon root data via ring buffer. 3. Verify EIP-4788 dependent contracts work correctly.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-35, Trust Boundary: tb-004. Incorrect ordering causes beacon root unavailability.

checklist_item.title: Does history storage system transaction execute as second operation?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify apply_body calls process_unchecked_system_transaction(HISTORY_STORAGE_ADDRESS) second. 2. Test history data availability during transaction execution. 3. Verify contracts querying historical block hashes work correctly.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-36, Trust Boundary: tb-004. Incorrect ordering causes stale history data.

checklist_item.title: Are transactions processed in sequential order within a block?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify apply_body processes transactions in block transaction list order. 2. Test that out-of-order processing produces different state roots. 3. Verify sequential ordering is a consensus invariant. 4. Check for any parallelization that might violate ordering.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-37, Trust Boundary: tb-006. Out-of-order processing causes consensus failure.

checklist_item.title: Are withdrawals processed after all transactions?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify apply_body calls process_withdrawals after all transactions. 2. Test that premature withdrawal processing alters state visible to transactions. 3. Verify state root divergence if ordering is violated.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-38, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Premature processing causes state root divergence.

checklist_item.title: Are general purpose requests processed last in block body?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify apply_body calls process_general_purpose_requests after withdrawals and transactions. 2. Test that premature processing produces incorrect request data. 3. Verify requests_hash divergence if ordering is violated.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-39, Trust Boundary: tb-011. Incorrect ordering produces different requests_hash.

checklist_item.title: Are request types appended in correct order (deposits, withdrawals, consolidations)?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Craft transactions generating deposit logs, withdrawal requests, and consolidation requests. 2. Verify process_general_purpose_requests assembles in order: deposits, withdrawals, consolidations. 3. Test that incorrect ordering produces different requests_hash. 4. Verify consensus split if ordering differs between implementations.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-40, Trust Boundary: tb-011. Request ordering determines requests_hash.

checklist_item.title: Are deposit requests parsed from block output logs (not system transactions)?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify deposit requests are derived via parse_deposit_requests from log entries. 2. Test for incorrect contract address or ABI decoding. 3. Craft log entries attempting to inject fraudulent deposits. 4. Verify legitimate deposits are not missed.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-41, Trust Boundary: tb-011. Incorrect parsing could inject fraudulent validator deposits.

checklist_item.title: Are withdrawal and consolidation requests from checked system transactions?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify process_checked_system_transaction is called for withdrawal and consolidation request contracts. 2. Test what happens if checked is replaced with unchecked. 3. Manipulate system contract state to cause failures. 4. Verify failures are caught and not silently ignored.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-42, Trust Boundaries: tb-004, tb-011. Silent failures corrupt request data network-wide.

checklist_item.title: Are blocks with number < 1 rejected?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit block with header.number = 0 via P2P. 2. Verify validate_header rejects the block. 3. Test for chain state corruption if accepted. 4. Test for triggering genesis-specific code paths.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-43, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Block 0 could trigger unexpected code paths.

checklist_item.title: Are block timestamps strictly increasing?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit block with timestamp <= parent timestamp. 2. Verify validate_header rejects the block. 3. Test TIMESTAMP opcode behavior with non-increasing timestamps. 4. Verify time-locked contracts work correctly.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-44, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Non-increasing timestamps break time-dependent operations.

checklist_item.title: Are block numbers sequential (parent.number + 1)?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit block with number != parent.number + 1 (gap or duplicate). 2. Verify validate_header rejects the block. 3. Test for chain continuity breaks. 4. Test for block number reuse attacks.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-45, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Non-sequential numbers break chain continuity.

checklist_item.title: Does parent_hash cryptographically match parent header?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Create block with parent_hash != keccak256(rlp.encode(parent_header)). 2. Verify validate_header rejects the block. 3. Test for chain reorganization attacks. 4. Verify cryptographic hash verification is correct.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-47, Trust Boundaries: tb-002, tb-005. Mismatch enables chain reorganization attacks.

checklist_item.title: Does excess_blob_gas match calculation from parent?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit block with incorrect excess_blob_gas. 2. Verify validate_header rejects the block. 3. Test for arithmetic edge cases (underflow when target > used). 4. Verify client divergence on different arithmetic handling.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-48, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Incorrect value affects blob fee calculations.

checklist_item.title: Is gas_used <= gas_limit enforced in header?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Submit block with gas_used > gas_limit in header. 2. Verify validate_header rejects the block. 3. Test gas accounting consistency. 4. Verify consensus failure on gas_used root check.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-49, Trust Boundary: tb-005. Exceeding limit breaks gas accounting model.

checklist_item.title: Do system transactions originate from SYSTEM_ADDRESS (0xff...fe)?
checklist_item.test_procedure: 1. Verify process_system_transaction sets origin and caller to SYSTEM_ADDRESS. 2. Test for non-SYSTEM_ADDRESS caller scenarios. 3. Verify system contracts have caller-based access controls. 4. Test if incorrect caller bypasses access controls.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-52, Trust Boundary: tb-004. Incorrect address allows non-privileged callers.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does base fee calculation enforce minimum increase of 1 when gas exceeds target?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Identify the calculate_base_fee_per_gas function implementation.
2. Create test cases where parent_gas_used > parent_gas_target.
3. Verify base_fee_per_gas_delta is always >= 1 in these scenarios.
4. Test edge cases at exact boundary values.
5. Confirm the asymmetry between increase and decrease is correctly implemented.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-13, Trust Boundary: tb-012

checklist_item.title: Verify transaction validation check ordering: Are checks ordered from general to specific?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Review the transaction_validation_dispatch function.
2. Verify checks are ordered: type -> fees -> gas limits -> blob-specific -> authorization.
3. Submit transactions designed to fail at each validation stage.
4. Measure CPU time for invalid transactions at different stages.
5. Confirm cheap checks occur before expensive operations.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-14, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Conditional scope - requires further investigation for high-effort DoS.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is extra_data in block header limited to 32 bytes?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Locate the validate_header function implementation.
2. Create a block header with extra_data of exactly 32 bytes - verify acceptance.
3. Create a block header with extra_data of 33 bytes - verify rejection.
4. Test with extra_data of 0 bytes.
5. Verify consistent behavior across different block types.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-46, Trust Boundary: tb-005

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is gas limit change bounded by parent_gas_limit // 1024?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Locate the check_gas_limit function implementation.
2. Calculate the maximum allowed gas limit change for a given parent.
3. Test with gas_limit change at exactly the boundary - verify acceptance.
4. Test with gas_limit change exceeding the boundary - verify rejection.
5. Verify both increase and decrease directions are checked.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-50, Trust Boundary: tb-012

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is gas limit >= 5000 (GAS_LIMIT_MINIMUM) enforced?
checklist_item.test_procedure:
1. Locate the check_gas_limit function implementation.
2. Test with gas_limit = 5000 - verify acceptance.
3. Test with gas_limit = 4999 - verify rejection.
4. Test with gas_limit = 0 - verify rejection.
5. Confirm the GAS_LIMIT_MINIMUM constant is correctly defined.
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0B17-51, Trust Boundary: tb-012
